concept: Pillow / Soft Support for the Head or Body
description: A soft or yielding support placed under the head or another part of the body during sleep and rest. 'nuwera' (bed) is the prepared sleeping place; 'komalu' supports the smaller point where the resting body needs it.
sound_symbolism: The pillow's familiar place sounds first in 'koma' (head). Liquid 'lu' yields under that weight instead of ending in a hard surface.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia nia ha komalu koma thinu.' (I put my head on this pillow.) Its fill and firmness belong in ordinary modifiers. A cushion used for sitting remains 'meipa' (seat) when the cushion itself is the seat.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A pillow supports sleep without doing the sleeping; comfort can prepare rest but cannot command it.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Straw or wool made a small support from material the household could clean and renew. Feathers, folded cloth, and chaff answered the same need elsewhere.

tags.physical: a yielding support for the head or body
tags.spatial: a small support placed within a resting place
